top_issues.title: No verification commands between resource creation steps
top_issues.fix: : Add `az cosmosdb show`, `az group show`, and `az cosmosdb sql container show` verification commands after each creation step with expected output.

top_issues.title: Download instruction is unnecessarily complex
top_issues.fix: : Provide a direct terminal download command as the primary method: `curl -o products.json "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AzureCosmosDB/CosmicWorks/main/data/database-v4/product"`

top_issues.title: Verification step suggests CLI query that might not work
top_issues.fix: : Suggest generating a Python verification script instead, or document that the `cosmosdb-preview` extension is needed.

top_issues.title: No expected output shown for any agent mode interaction
top_issues.fix: : Add sample output snippets or success indicators after each prompt (for example, "Agent mode runs the command and you see `provisioningState: Succeeded`").

top_issues.title: No time estimate for Cosmos DB account creation
top_issues.fix: : Add a note: "This step takes 3–5 minutes to complete."

top_issues.title: Missing RBAC permissions in prerequisites
top_issues.fix: : Add "Contributor role or higher on the subscription" to prerequisites.

top_issues.title: No cost warning
top_issues.fix: : Add a note about provisioned throughput costs and the importance of cleaning up.

top_issues.title: "Plan the work" section mixes educational and actionable content
top_issues.fix: : Clarify this is a "don't do this" example with explicit framing.

top_issues.title: Partition key `/id` is atypical for CosmicWorks data
top_issues.fix: : Consider using `/categoryId` or note that `/id` is used for simplicity in this quickstart.

whats_working: Clear prompt decomposition strategy — The "Plan the work" section provides excellent guidance on breaking large tasks into atomic prompts, which is a genuine best practice for agent mode.
Correct Azure CLI commands — All underlying CLI operations (resource group, Cosmos DB account, database, container creation) use valid, current syntax that executes without modification.
Valid data source — The CosmicWorks GitHub URL is active and returns 295 well-structured JSON product items with proper `id` fields.
Complete lifecycle coverage — The doc covers setup through cleanup, ensuring readers don't leave orphaned Azure resources.
Multi-shell tab support — Python venv instructions cover PowerShell, Git Bash, and macOS/Linux in a tabbed format, accommodating different development environments.

steps.name: Create a new directory on local computer
Open VS Code from that local directory
Open GitHub Copilot chat and switch to agent mode
Download products data (products.json from CosmicWorks repo)
Sign in to Azure tenant and choose subscription
Create a resource group in West US region
Create an Azure Cosmos DB for NoSQL account
Create a database in the Cosmos DB account
Create a container with partition key /id
Generate Python code to upload JSON items to the container
Verify the import by querying first 5 items

verification_steps.current: "Make sure to rename the file to products.json"
"Which tenant and subscription am I working with?"
verification_steps.recommended: `az account show --query name -o tsv` → your subscription name
`az cosmosdb sql database show --account-name <acct> -g <rg> -n <db> --query name -o tsv`
`az cosmosdb sql container show ... --query resource.partitionKey.paths[0] -o tsv` → `/id`
Python query returning 5 items with name/price fields
